assertions.assertion: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Repository Registry' section with a table listing both 'trustify-backend' (serena_backend) and 'trustify-ui' (serena_ui) as entries
assertions.evidence: Found '## Repository Registry' at line 20 with a table containing two rows: 'trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend' (line 24) and 'trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui' (line 25).

assertions.assertion: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Jira Configuration' section with all 5 fields: Project key (TC), Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID (10142), Git Pull Request custom field (customfield_10875), GitHub Issue custom field (customfield_10747)
assertions.evidence: Found '## Jira Configuration' at line 27 with all 5 fields: Project key: TC (line 29), Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432 (line 30), Feature issue type ID: 10142 (line 31), Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875 (line 32), GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747 (line 33).

assertions.assertion: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Code Intelligence' section that documents the mcp__<instance>__<tool> naming convention
assertions.evidence: Found '## Code Intelligence' at line 35. Line 37 states: 'Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name: `mcp__<instance>__<tool>`.' Additionally includes concrete examples for both serena_backend and serena_ui instances.

assertions.assertion: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '### Limitations' subheading under Code Intelligence
assertions.evidence: Found '### Limitations' at line 55, appearing after the '## Code Intelligence' section (line 35) and before '## Bug Configuration' (line 59). Content states: 'No limitations known for either Serena instance.'

assertions.assertion: outputs/discovery-log.md mentions discovering 2 Serena instances (serena_backend and serena_ui) from the MCP tool listing
assertions.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 9-12: 'Two Serena instances were identified from the available MCP tools: 1. serena_backend — Discovered from tools prefixed with mcp__serena_backend__ ... 2. serena_ui — Discovered from tools prefixed with mcp__serena_ui__'. Source noted as 'MCP tools listing (mcp-tools-with-serena.md)'.

assertions.assertion: outputs/changes-log.md indicates all Project Configuration sections (Repository Registry, Jira Configuration, Code Intelligence, Bug Configuration) were newly added — pre-existing non-configuration content from the CLAUDE.md (like the project heading and Documentation section) may appear as preserved, which is expected since only Project Configuration was missing
assertions.evidence: changes-log.md 'Preserved' section (lines 3-10) lists existing content: top-level heading, description, Documentation section, Getting Started section. 'Added' section (lines 12-38) lists all four Project Configuration sections as newly added under '# Project Configuration': Repository Registry, Jira Configuration, Code Intelligence, Bug Configuration (plus Hierarchy Configuration).

assertions.assertion: The Repository Registry table contains columns: Repository, Role, Serena Instance, Path — matching project-config.template.md structure
assertions.evidence: Line 22 of claude-md-result.md shows the table header: '| Repository | Role | Serena Instance | Path |' — exact match to the expected column structure.

assertions.assertion: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Bug Configuration' section with three fields: Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks)
assertions.evidence: Found '## Bug Configuration' at line 59. Three fields present: 'Bug issue type ID: 10001' (line 61), 'Bug template: docs/bug-template.md' (line 62), 'Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks' (line 63).

assertions.assertion: The skill offers to enable security triage for the project (Step 9 opt-in prompt) and respects the user's decline — outputs/claude-md-result.md does NOT contain a '## Security Configuration' section
assertions.evidence: claude-md-result.md does not contain any '## Security Configuration' section. discovery-log.md confirms the prompt was offered and declined (lines 52-56: 'User declined to enable security triage for this project. Security Configuration section was not added.'). changes-log.md confirms it was skipped (lines 42-43: 'Security Configuration - Not added — user declined security triage enablement').
